title: 11 Herbal Essential Oils For Swollen Gums
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils can be really helpful in soothing swollen gums. When you drink herbal teas, the active ingredients in the herbs get absorbed into your body, which helps to reduce swelling and fight off the underlying infection. For example, Eucalyptus globulus oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can ease pain and swelling in your gums. It's also a natural antibacterial agent, which means it can help clear up infections and prevent them from coming back.  Echinacea purpurea oil is another great example - it's known for its immune-boosting properties, which can help your body fight off infections and reduce inflammation in your gums. Plus, it's been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease pain. And then there's Salvia officinalis oil, which has been used for centuries to calm and soothe irritated tissues, including swollen gums. Its antibacterial properties can also help prevent infections and promote healing.  Drinking herbal teas or using these essential oils can bring a lot of benefits to your life. For one, it can help you avoid taking harsh medications or undergoing painful treatments. It can also help you feel more relaxed and in control when dealing with swollen gums. And, by using natural remedies, you can avoid exposing yourself to harsh chemicals or artificial additives that can have negative side effects. By incorporating herbal essential oils into your daily routine, you can take a proactive approach to maintaining good oral health and preventing future problems.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for swollen gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils contains compounds like eucalyptol and alpha-pinene, which are known for their anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce swelling and pain in swollen gums. Eucalyptol, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, which contribute to gum inflammation. The antimicrobial properties of eucalyptus globulus essential oils also help combat the bacteria that can cause gum infections and inflammation. By reducing inflammation and fighting infection, eucalyptus globulus essential oils can help alleviate swollen gums.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal essential oils for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Eucalyptus globulus leaves and 1 cup of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over medium heat and add the Eucalyptus leaves. Let it simmer for 30-40 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a bowl using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve. Discard the solids.
Transfer the liquid to a clean glass bottle and let it cool. Use 10-15 drops of the essential oil in a carrier oil like coconut oil for gum treatment.
Store the Eucalyptus globulus essential oil in a cool, dark place and use it within 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils contains bioactive constituents like alkylamides and caffeic acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, making it a good remedy for swollen gums. The caffeic acid in Echinacea purpurea essential oils also has antioxidant properties, which help protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals. The antiseptic properties of Echinacea purpurea essential oils help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum swelling. By using Echinacea purpurea essential oils, you may be able to reduce the swelling and inflammation of your gums naturally.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oils for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea flowers and 2 cups of carrier oil (such as coconut or olive oil).
Combine the flowers and carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Stir well.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Discard the solids and use the remaining liquid as Echinacea purpurea essential oil for swollen gums. Store in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis essential oils contains a compound called thujone, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling. This oil also has tannins, which are astringent and can help tighten gum tissues, reducing swelling and discomfort. Additionally, Salvia officinalis essential oils contain flavonoids, such as rosmarinic acid, that have antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing. The antiseptic properties of the oil, due to the presence of triterpenoids and saponins, can also help prevent infections that can cause swollen gums. When used properly, Salvia officinalis essential oils can be an effective natural remedy for swollen gums, especially when combined with good oral hygiene practices.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal essential oils for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves and 2 cups of water in a saucepan. Heat the water over low heat.
Add a tablespoon of carrier oil (like jojoba or coconut oil) to a small bowl.
Pour the infused water from the saucepan into a glass jar. Let it cool for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid into the bowl with carrier oil using a coffee filter or cheesecloth. Discard the solids.
Label and store the Salvia officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle. Use 2-3 drops as needed for swollen gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains a compound called gingerol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the gums. Gingerol also contains shogaol and zingerone, which have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in soothing swollen gums. These bioactive constituents work by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which contribute to inflammation and swelling. By reducing inflammation and pain, Zingiber officinale essential oils can help alleviate the discomfort associated with swollen gums. Regular use of this essential oil may lead to a reduction in swelling and improve oral health.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Zingiber officinale (Ginger) roots, 1 cup of water, and a steam distillation kit.
Wash the ginger roots with water and chop them into small pieces.
Combine the chopped ginger with 1 cup of water in the steam distillation kit.
Heat the mixture over low heat, then collect the steam and direct it into the distillation kit.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the water and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils contains a high concentration of compounds like cineole and terpinen-4-ol, which are known for their antimicrobial properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and combat bacteria that can cause swollen gums. The oils' antibacterial properties also help to fight off infections that can cause gum swelling. Cineole, in particular, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe swollen tissues. This combination of antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties makes Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils a useful treatment for swollen gums.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal essential oils for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Melaleuca alternifolia leaves. Clean and dry them.
Steep 1 cup of the leaves in 1 cup of carrier oil, like jojoba oil, in a glass jar for 2 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container. Discard the solids.
Store the oil in a dark glass bottle in a cool, dry place.
Use 2-3 drops of the essential oil mixed with water as a mouthwash 2-3 times a day for swollen gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils contains camphor, borneol, and bornyl acetate. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce swelling in the gums. The camphor in Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils has a cooling effect, which can help ease inflammation and discomfort in the gums. Bornyl acetate has a sedative effect, which can help calm the mouth and reduce pain. The antimicrobial properties of Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils help combat the bacteria that cause gum infections and inflammation, promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over medium heat until it starts boiling.
Add the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves to the boiling water and reduce heat to low.
Steep the leaves for 2 hours, then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl.
Transfer the liquid to a dark glass bottle and let it sit at room temperature for 24 hours before using it as a swollen gum remedy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains linalool and linalyl acetate, which are its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe swollen gums. Linalool in particular has been shown to reduce redness and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of linalyl acetate help prevent infections that can exacerbate gum swelling. By using Lavandula angustifolia essential oils, you can create a natural and effective remedy for swollen gums.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers, a glass jar with a lid, a cup of water, and a clean cloth.
Wash the Lavandula angustifolia flowers with 1 cup of water, then pat them dry with a clean cloth.
Fill the glass jar with 1 cup of the dried flowers, and pour 2 cups of water over them.
Cover the jar with a lid and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a clean cloth into a bowl, discard the solids, and store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oils contains thymol and carvacrol, which are powerful antimicrobial agents that help reduce swelling in gums. Thymol has anti-inflammatory properties that inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which contribute to swelling and pain. Carvacrol, on the other hand, has antiseptic properties that help prevent infections that can cause gum inflammation. The antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties of thymol and carvacrol in Thymus vulgaris essential oils help reduce swelling and promote healing in swollen gums. When applied topically, these oils can help alleviate gum inflammation and promote oral health.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal essential oils for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus vulgaris leaves and 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil).
Wash the leaves with water to remove dirt. Pat them dry with a paper towel.
In a blender or food processor, blend the leaves into a fine paste.
Transfer the paste to a clean glass jar and mix it with the carrier oil. Store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.

After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container. Discard the solids and use the remaining liquid as your Thymus vulgaris essential oil for swollen gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils contains high levels of menthol and menthone, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. These properties make it effective in reducing swelling and pain in swollen gums. The menthol helps to numb the area and reduce inflammation, while the menthone kills bacteria that can cause infections. The essential oil also contains limonene, which has antiseptic properties that help to prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi. When used topically, Mentha x piperita essential oils can help to soothe and calm swollen gums, promoting a healthy environment for healing.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Mentha x piperita (peppermint) leaves and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat, then add the peppermint leaves and let it simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the solids.
Let the mixture sit at room temperature for 2-3 hours to allow the steam to rise and the oil to separate.
Collect the separated oil using a pipette or a dropper and transfer it to a dark glass bottle for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils contains bioactive constituents like curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce swelling and inflammation in the body, including swollen gums. Curcumin, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which contribute to gum inflammation. The antioxidant properties of curcuma longa essential oils also help protect the gums from oxidative stress, promoting healthy gum tissue. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of curcuma longa essential oils make it a useful treatment for swollen gums.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal essential oils for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh turmeric roots (Curcuma longa). Clean and dry them.
Grind the turmeric roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Add 2 cups of carrier oil (like coconut or jojoba oil) to the ground turmeric powder. Mix well.
Transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily.

After 2 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container. Discard the solids. Your Curcuma longa essential oil is now ready to use. Use 2-3 drops as needed for swollen gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils contains limonene, linalool, and geranial as its key bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce swelling and fight off the bacteria causing gum infections. The antioxidant properties of limonene and linalool also help protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals. Geranial's antiseptic properties further aid in preventing the growth of bacteria and promoting healing. By using Cymbopogon citratus essential oils, you can effectively combat swollen gums due to its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves and 2 cups of water in a pot.
Heat the water over low heat and add the leaves. Let it simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl. Discard the solids.
Add 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil) to the liquid and stir well.
Let the mixture sit for 2-3 hours or overnight before straining it again. Use the resulting essential oil for swollen gums.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent swollen gums from forming. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the gums. They may also reduce stress and improve oral health. Regularly drinking these teas might keep your gums healthy and prevent swelling.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can help with swollen gums, but it's essential to use them in moderation. Drinking too much tea daily can lead to an imbalance of certain nutrients in your body. You can try having a cup of herbal tea a few times a week to see if it helps. Just be aware of how your body reacts.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with swollen gums. The time it takes to see results varies, but you might notice improvements in a few days to a week. Some people find their gums feel better after a few sips of tea, while others might not notice a difference until they've been drinking it regularly for a week or so.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for swollen gums is best in the morning or early afternoon. At this time, your body is more likely to absorb the beneficial compounds from the tea, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing. This timing allows the tea to work effectively before bedtime.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for swollen gums. Some herbs, like ginger and turmeric, can slow blood clotting, which may affect medications that also do this. This could lead to increased bleeding. Other herbs, like ginkgo, may increase the risk of side effects from certain medications. It's best to talk to a doctor or pharmacist about any interactions.
faq_pregnant: Herbal teas can be a soothing remedy for swollen gums, but it's essential to be cautious when pregnant. Some herbal teas, like chamomile and peppermint, are considered safe in moderation. However, others like sage and echinacea may stimulate the uterus and cause issues. If you're pregnant, choose gentle, well-known herbal teas and drink them in small amounts.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a soothing option for swollen gums while breastfeeding. However, it's essential to check the ingredients. Some teas, like chamomile and peppermint, are often safe for nursing mothers. But others, like sage and pennyroyal, contain compounds that might affect milk production or be passed to the baby. Choose teas known to be safe during breastfeeding.
title_2: Older Adults with Swollen Gums: How 11 Herbal Essential Oils Can Help
